extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters initiated by versus against each entity: I counted only the “Against” rows, with criminal proceedings nil for the Company, Directors, Promoters and Subsidiaries; statutory/regulatory proceedings nil across those rows; and tax proceedings of 2 against the Company plus 2 against the Subsidiaries, excluding all “By” rows and promoter personal matters. For the aggregate amount against the consolidated group, I used the Company amount of ₹1,007.38 million plus the Subsidiaries amount of ₹1,010.08 million, totaling ₹2,017.46 million, or ₹201.746 crore; the source table is in ₹ million and the conversion is million ÷ 10. The contingent-liabilities table is dated June 30, 2023 and lists ₹201.99 million of bank guarantees and ₹8.83 million of disputed statutory demands but provides no stated Total row, so the total field is not extracted under the stated rule. No company-wide top-five or top-ten customer revenue concentration percentage is disclosed; the percentages shown concern top products or business channels, not customers. The auditor disclosures mention an internal-financial-controls disclaimer and an emphasis-of-matter/CARO qualification, but no modified opinion on the financial statements themselves; going concern is not mentioned.
